question.text: Which factor has a DIFFERENT directional effect on call option premium versus put option premium?

answer.text: Spot price - an increase raises call premium but decreases put premium
Volatility - an increase raises both call and put premiums equally
Time to expiry - more time raises both call and put premiums
Lot size - a larger lot size raises both call and put contract values

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Spot price effect: - Call premium INCREASES when spot price rises (closer to/deeper ITM) - Put premium DECREASES when spot price rises (closer to/deeper OTM)
Volatility and time to expiry affect both calls and puts in the SAME direction (increase premiums).
**The Trap:** Interest rates also have differential effects (increase helps calls, hurts puts), but the effect is much smaller than spot price changes.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Delta captures the spot price sensitivity: positive for calls, negative for puts.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Spot price up: call premium up, put premium down. Volatility up: both premiums up.
